title: Meditation and Yoga for Irritable Bowel Syndrome: A Randomized Clinical Trial
headline: Meditation and Yoga for Irritable Bowel Syndrome: A Randomized Clinical Trial
summary: A randomized controlled trial of yoga examined stress & anxiety. The evidence for efficacy, feasibility, and safety of virtually delivered yoga for patients with IBS is unknown. No significant difference was found between groups in IBS-SSS score postintervention ( P = 0.149), but significant differences in favor of the treatment group for quality of life ( P = 0.030), fatigue ( P = 0.035), and perceived stress ( P = 0.040) were identified.
keyFindings: The evidence for efficacy, feasibility, and safety of virtually delivered yoga for patients with IBS is unknown.

No significant difference was found between groups in IBS-SSS score postintervention ( P = 0.149), but significant differences in favor of the treatment group for quality of life ( P = 0.030), fatigue ( P = 0.035), and perceived stress ( P = 0.040) were identified.
limitations: Single-trial result; requires replication, and self-reported outcomes may inflate apparent effects.
The practice studied (yoga) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
